input.command:
echo "=== all namespaces ==="; kubectl get ns
echo "=== mongodb secrets ==="; kubectl -n mongodb get secrets
echo "=== elasticsearch secrets ==="; kubectl -n elasticsearch get secrets
echo "=== any other namespaces with relevant resources ==="
kubectl get secrets -A | grep -i report
kubectl get configmaps -A | grep -i report

input.command:
echo "=== es-file-realm-aggregate ==="
kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret es-file-realm-aggregate -o json | python3 -c "
import sys,json,base64
d=json.load(sys.stdin)
for k,v in d['data'].items():
    print('---',k,'---')
    print(base64.b64decode(v).decode(errors='replace'))
" 2>&1 || kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret es-file-realm-aggregate -o jsonpath='{.data}' 
echo
echo "=== user-provided-file-realm ==="
kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret user-provided-file-realm -o jsonpath='{.data}'

echo "=== decode users files ==="
echo "ops-user hash line:"; kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret es-file-realm-aggregate -o jsonpath='{.data.users}' | base64 -d
echo "ops roles:"; kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret es-file-realm-aggregate -o jsonpath='{.data.users_roles}' | base64 -d
echo "report-user hash line:"; kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret user-provided-file-realm -o jsonpath='{.data.users}' | base64 -d
echo "report roles:"; kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret user-provided-file-realm -o jsonpath='{.data.users_roles}' | base64 -d
echo
echo "=== current StatefulSet spec (volumes/mounts) ==="
kubectl -n elasticsearch get statefulset es-node -o yaml | grep -A5 -i "volumeMounts\|volumes:" 
echo
echo "=== configmap es-config ==="
kubectl -n elasticsearch get configmap es-config -o jsonpath='{.data.elasticsearch\.yml}'

=== configmap es-config ===
cluster.name: es-cluster
node.name: ${POD_NAME}
network.host: 0.0.0.0
http.port: 9200
transport.port: 9300
discovery.seed_hosts:
  - es-discovery
cluster.initial_master_nodes:
  - es-node-0
  - es-node-1
  - es-node-2
node.roles: ["master", "data", "ingest"]
path.data: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data

ingest.geoip.downloader.enabled: false

xpack.security.enabled: true
xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: certs/tls.crt
xpack.security.http.ssl.key: certs/tls.key
xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: ["certs/ca.crt"]
xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: certs/tls.crt
xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: certs/tls.key
xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: ["certs/ca.crt"]

input.command: kubectl -n elasticsearch get statefulset es-node -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.volumes}' | python3 2>/dev/null || kubectl -n elasticsearch get statefulset es-node -o json | grep -A 30 '"volumes"'

echo "=== all pods all namespaces ==="
kubectl get pods -A -o wide
echo "=== all secrets all namespaces ==="
kubectl get secrets -A
echo "=== events in elasticsearch ns ==="
kubectl -n elasticsearch get events --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -30
echo "=== events in mongodb ns ==="
kubectl -n mongodb get events --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -20

input.command:
kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret es-http-tls -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.crt}' | base64 -d > /tmp/ca.crt
kubectl -n elasticsearch cp /tmp/ca.crt curl-test:/tmp/ca.crt 2>&1
kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -s --cacert /tmp/ca.crt -u elastic:elasticpass "https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/role/viewer?pretty"
echo "=== check current realms / security settings ==="
kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -s --cacert /tmp/ca.crt -u elastic:elasticpass "https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate?pretty"

content.text: `viewer` and `superuser` are both built-in reserved ES roles — no custom role definition needed. The root cause is clear: file-realm credential files (`users`/`users_roles`/`roles.yml`) were never mounted into the ES pods at all, so neither `ops-user` nor the new `report-user` can authenticate. Let me check the `file-realm-gen` pod for any leftover artifacts (e.g. plaintext passwords used to generate the hashes) before I fix this.

input.command: kubectl -n elasticsearch exec file-realm-gen -- sh -c "cat ~/.bash_history 2>/dev/null; find / -maxdepth 4 -newer /usr/share/elasticsearch/bin/elasticsearch -type f 2>/dev/null | grep -v -E '^/(proc|sys)' | head -50"

input.subject: Merge file-realm secrets and wire into StatefulSet
input.description: Merge es-file-realm-aggregate (ops-user) and user-provided-file-realm (report-user) into a single merged secret, mount users/users_roles/roles.yml into the es-node StatefulSet without disturbing existing native realm users.

content.content: Task #2 created successfully: Merge file-realm secrets and wire into StatefulSet

task.subject: Merge file-realm secrets and wire into StatefulSet

input.subject: Roll out change and verify cluster health + auth
input.description: Apply StatefulSet update, confirm rolling update keeps cluster healthy, verify file-realm users are registered and native elastic user still authenticates.

content.content: Task #3 created successfully: Roll out change and verify cluster health + auth

task.subject: Roll out change and verify cluster health + auth

content.content: Updated task #1 status
Updated task #2 status

content.text: Root cause confirmed: `es-file-realm-aggregate` (has `ops-user`/superuser) and `user-provided-file-realm` (has the new `report-user`/viewer) were both created, but the ES StatefulSet never mounts any file-realm files at all — so file-realm auth was never wired in. Let's build the merged secret preserving both existing hashes exactly.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/users <<'EOF'
ops-user:$2a$10$51WqGILZx37jA5zy7BZtruMmcf4tOSmt/Lbg.1XkKpwWJt7K8Ppz.
report-user:$2a$10$NfTSFau/06FRumEztq7NwOTT/Nt7WZ6tdNfSdOfB6QrniCAuchBfi
EOF
cat > /tmp/users_roles <<'EOF'
superuser:ops-user
viewer:report-user
EOF
: > /tmp/roles.yml
cat /tmp/users; echo "---"; cat /tmp/users_roles; echo "---roles.yml empty---"

content.content:
ops-user:$2a$10$51WqGILZx37jA5zy7BZtruMmcf4tOSmt/Lbg.1XkKpwWJt7K8Ppz.
report-user:$2a$10$NfTSFau/06FRumEztq7NwOTT/Nt7WZ6tdNfSdOfB6QrniCAuchBfi
---
superuser:ops-user
viewer:report-user
---roles.yml empty---

input.command:
kubectl -n elasticsearch create secret generic es-file-realm-aggregate \
  --from-file=users=/tmp/users \
  --from-file=users_roles=/tmp/users_roles \
  --from-file=roles.yml=/tmp/roles.yml \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
echo "=== verify ==="
kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret es-file-realm-aggregate -o jsonpath='{.data.users}' | base64 -d
kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret es-file-realm-aggregate -o jsonpath='{.data.users_roles}' | base64 -d

content.content:
secret/es-file-realm-aggregate configured
=== verify ===
ops-user:$2a$10$51WqGILZx37jA5zy7BZtruMmcf4tOSmt/Lbg.1XkKpwWJt7K8Ppz.
report-user:$2a$10$NfTSFau/06FRumEztq7NwOTT/Nt7WZ6tdNfSdOfB6QrniCAuchBfi
superuser:ops-user
viewer:report-user

content.text: Now let's patch the StatefulSet to mount these file-realm files into the ES config directory.

input.command:
kubectl -n elasticsearch get statefulset es-node -o yaml > /tmp/es-node-current.yaml
wc -l /tmp/es-node-current.yaml

content.content: 174 /tmp/es-node-current.yaml
File created successfully at: /tmp/es-file-realm-patch.json (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.command: kubectl -n elasticsearch patch statefulset es-node --type strategic --patch-file /tmp/es-file-realm-patch.json

content.content: statefulset.apps/es-node patched
